top_3_gaps: Motion and easing curves (NodeBench feels linear and stiff, especially the bottom sheet at 0:20)
Composer crowding (NodeBench crams 'Auto', '+', mic, and send into a tight space above a bottom nav)
Top chrome density (NodeBench has too many actions: back, title dropdown, search, plus, menu)
top_3_wins: Exact feature parity on the three-dot menu actions
Clear, structured task progress UI block

top_chrome.manus: Minimal: Back, Title, Share, Menu. Clean spacing.
top_chrome.nodebench: Heavy: Back, Title Dropdown, Search, Plus, Menu. Feels cramped.
top_chrome.delta: NodeBench is overloading the top navigation bar, reducing the premium feel.

composer_pinning.manus: Pinned cleanly to the bottom safe area, maximizing chat space.
composer_pinning.nodebench: Pinned above a persistent bottom navigation bar.
composer_pinning.delta: NodeBench loses significant vertical real estate to the double-stack of composer + bottom nav.

bottom_nav.manus: Hidden in this deep-linked chat view, focusing entirely on the conversation.
bottom_nav.delta: NodeBench feels like you are still in the app shell rather than immersed in a task.

safe_area_handling.manus: Excellent, respects iOS home indicator perfectly.
safe_area_handling.nodebench: Acceptable, but the bottom nav makes the interactive area sit awkwardly high on the screen.
safe_area_handling.delta: NodeBench's interactive center of gravity is pushed up.

header_treatment.manus: Strong, bold headers that clearly delineate sections.
header_treatment.nodebench: Adequate, but lacks the crisp weight contrast seen in Manus.
header_treatment.delta: NodeBench feels slightly more generic, lacking typographic punch.

body_readability.manus: High readability, excellent line-height and tracking.
body_readability.nodebench: Good, but text in the task progress block feels slightly dense.
body_readability.delta: Minor line-height adjustments needed in NodeBench.

weight_hierarchy.manus: Uses medium/semibold weights effectively to guide the eye.
weight_hierarchy.nodebench: A bit flat; everything feels like regular or medium weight.
weight_hierarchy.delta: NodeBench needs stricter rules for when to use bold vs regular.

transitions.nodebench: Linear, slightly stiff. Lacks the 'bounce' of a native app.
transitions.delta: NodeBench betrays its non-native or less-polished roots through its animation curves.

loading_states.manus: Subtle inline spinners and skeleton states.
loading_states.delta: NodeBench's approach is actually quite informative, but the animation of the progress steps is abrupt.

menu_open_close.manus: Smooth slide up with a nice background fade.
menu_open_close.nodebench: At 0:20, the 'Thread actions' sheet slides up with a very linear curve and the background dimming is harsh.
menu_open_close.delta: NodeBench feels like a web modal, Manus feels like a native bottom sheet.

motion.feel_rating: Manus: snappy. NodeBench: acceptable (borderline janky on sheet presentation).

palette.delta: NodeBench is slightly washed out compared to Manus's OLED-friendly blacks.

text_contrast.manus: High contrast, crisp white on black.
text_contrast.nodebench: Good contrast, but secondary text could use a slight bump in brightness.
text_contrast.delta: NodeBench secondary text is slightly muddy against the dark gray background.

accent_usage.manus: Restricted, purposeful use of color (e.g., green checkmarks).
accent_usage.nodebench: Blue accents for active states, red for delete. Standard but effective.

composer.delta: NodeBench's composer is overcrowded. The 'Auto' toggle and extra send arrow make the tap targets dangerously small.

artifact_rail.manus_presence: Horizontal scrolling file attachments above composer.
artifact_rail.delta: NodeBench lacks the rich artifact preview surface seen in Manus.

clarity_of_entry_point.nodebench: Clear, but distracted by the bottom nav and heavy top chrome.

visual_hierarchy.manus: Agent responses and user prompts are clearly separated.
visual_hierarchy.nodebench: The 'Task Progress' block dominates the screen, pushing actual conversational content down.
visual_hierarchy.delta: NodeBench over-indexes on showing the 'work' rather than the 'result'.

cognitive_load.nodebench: Medium-High. Too many icons in the top right, too many buttons in the composer.
cognitive_load.delta: NodeBench requires more visual parsing from the user.

tap_target_sizes.nodebench: The icons inside the composer (Auto, +, mic, send) are packed too tightly. High risk of mis-taps.
tap_target_sizes.delta: NodeBench fails basic mobile tap target heuristics in the composer.

reduced_motion_support.nodebench: Unknown, but current animations are already quite linear.

prioritized_punch_list.problem: Too many actions crammed into the input bar, resulting in tiny tap targets.
prioritized_punch_list.fix: Move 'Auto' out of the text field. Hide the send arrow until text is entered (replace mic with send dynamically).

prioritized_punch_list.problem: The 'Thread actions' menu at 0:20 slides up with a linear, cheap-feeling animation.
prioritized_punch_list.fix: Apply a standard iOS ease-out spring curve (e.g., damping 0.8, stiffness 250) to the sheet presentation.

prioritized_punch_list.problem: Header is cluttered with Search, Plus, and Menu icons next to a dropdown title.
prioritized_punch_list.fix: Consolidate actions. Move 'Search' and 'Plus' into the three-dot menu, or remove the title dropdown if unnecessary.

honest_assessment.can_nodebench_demo_tomorrow: Yes. The app is functionally complete, the UI is clean enough not to distract, and it achieves feature parity on core menus.
honest_assessment.biggest_risk_in_demo: The app feeling like a cheap web-wrapper due to the stiff bottom sheet animation and the overcrowded, non-native feeling composer bar.
quick_wins_before_demo: Tweak the CSS/JS animation curve on the bottom sheet to ease-out.
Increase horizontal padding between icons in the composer.
Remove at least one icon from the top right header to let it breathe.
